summary: Large unmerged batch PR collecting 94 matched or improved functions from multiple subdirectory worktrees across camera, effects, fighter character code, library helpers, menu/name-entry, map, trophy/toy, and vi/un modules. The branch converted several objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, added many function bodies and header signature updates, and included build/review-fix commits. It was closed almost immediately because the batch was too large, with the author stating they would resubmit smaller focused PRs of 5-7 functions each.
what_changed: Converted multiple source objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, including lbcardnew.c, lbsnap.c, eflib.c, ftKb_Init.c, ftPp_SpecialS.c, mnname.c, toy.c, and tyfigupon.c.

Added or improved many camera matches in src/melee/cm/camera.c, including Camera_8002B0E0, Camera_8002E6FC, Camera_8002E818, Camera_8002E948, Camera_8002EB5C, Camera_8002EC7C, Camera_8002ED9C, Camera_8002F274, Camera_8002F9E4, and Camera_8002FC7C.

Matched many effect-library functions in eflib.c and efasync.c, with associated header signature updates for functions such as efLib_8005C2BC, efLib_8005F774, efLib_8005CB34, efLib_8005E2B4, efLib_8005C1B4, and efLib_8005F454.
Worked on fighter character files for Kirby and Popo, including ftKb_Init functions and Popo SpecialHi/SpecialS-related functions, with missing prototypes and header corrections.
Matched library functions in lb modules, including lbSnap_8001DF6C, lb_8001C4A8, lbRefract_8002247C, lbAudioAx, lbMthp, and lbRefract-related functions.
Updated mnname.c name-entry helpers including mnName_802385A0, IsNameListFull, GetNameCount, GetNameText, and IsNameValid.
Updated mp island/lib code, including mpIsland_8005B334, mpIsland_8005AE1C, mpLib_80053ECC_Floor, mpisland.h signatures, and a struct layout update.
Added trophy/toy matches across ty/toy.c, tyfigupon.c, tydisplay.c, and tylist.c, with build fixes for missing includes and prototypes.
Added many vi/un matches across vi0401.c, vi0801.c, vi1101.c, vi1201v1.c, vi1201v2.c, and vi1202.c, including several vi1202 functions and some partial fuzzy matches noted in the PR body.
Replaced orig/GALE01/sys/.gitkeep with a symlink to /tmp/melee_extract/sys in the diff excerpt; this was part of the PR diff but not described as a deliberate decomp change.
smart_moves: Grouped function-match work by subdirectory in the PR body, making the otherwise large batch easier to scan by system.

Recorded match percentages in commit summaries, distinguishing 100% matches from partial improvements such as Camera_8002FC7C at 98.2%, Camera_8002F9E4 at 85.4%, efAsync_8006737C at 93.3%, fn_800204C8 at 91.9%, IsNameValid at 97.8%, and vi1202 partial fuzzy matches.
Paired matching commits with header signature updates, which is important when source bodies only match after prototypes, return types, or argument types reflect the original ABI.
Included build-fix commits when subdirectory work exposed missing prototypes, incorrect calls, missing includes, or files that needed to remain NonMatching.
The author closed the PR after recognizing reviewability problems and stated an intent to resubmit smaller focused PRs, preserving the work while aligning with repository review expectations.
decomp_lessons: Do not submit very large mixed-system matching batches even if many individual functions match; this PR was closed because 94 functions was too large to review effectively.
Prefer focused PRs of approximately 5-7 functions, as explicitly stated by the author when closing this batch.
When flipping files from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, keep the PR narrow enough that object-status changes can be audited alongside the exact matched functions.
Header signatures and source matches should be developed together; several commits in this batch specifically updated function signatures before or after matching bodies.
Partial matches should be clearly labeled with match percentages or fuzzy scores so reviewers and future workers know what is complete versus still approximate.

Build validation and build-fix commits are valuable, but bundling many independent systems makes it harder to isolate which signature, include, or object-status change caused a build or matching issue.
Avoid committing local extraction artifacts or machine-specific symlinks such as orig/GALE01/sys -> /tmp/melee_extract/sys unless the repository explicitly expects them.
naming_conventions: Existing symbol-address names were preserved for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions, such as Camera_8002E6FC, efLib_8005BBB4, efAsync_8006730C, mnName_802385A0, and un_80321AF4.
Unknown UI/vi/toy functions used the existing un_ or fn_ prefix conventions, for example un_8031B9A4_OnFrame, un_803122D0_OnInit, fn_80315574, and fn_803219AC.
Lifecycle suffixes such as _OnFrame and _OnInit were used where behavior was known enough to encode callback role.
Header fixes retained module prefixes matching file/system ownership, such as ftKb_, ftPp_, efLib_, lbSnap_, lbRefract_, mpIsland_, tyFigupon_, and Camera_.
Temporary or matching-helper names appear in the diff excerpt, including new_var, x304_ptr, x32C_ptr, pause_eye_ptr, and X35C_BITS; these reflect matching work rather than stable semantic naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used pointer temporaries to influence codegen and load/store ordering, as shown in Camera_8002F9E4 and Camera_8002FC7C with x304_ptr, x32C_ptr, x314_ptr, pause_eye_ptr, and transform.
Used explicit casts to packed storage or raw offsets in camera globals, such as *(s32*)&cm_80452C68.pad_342[2], *(Vec3*)&cm_80452C68.pad_342[2], and byte-offset writes around x35C.
Introduced a small bitfield helper struct and macro, X35C_Bits and X35C_BITS, to manipulate individual bits inside cm_80452C68.x35C in camera functions.
Preserved apparently redundant or forced operations for matching, such as the diff comment 'cm_803BCCA0; // Force load' in Camera_8002F9E4.
Adjusted integer masks and casts to match generated code, for example changing Player_GetPlayerId(0) masking from 0xFFFFu to 0xFFu in Camera_8002B0E0.
In efasync.c, changed boolean/pointer tests to integer ORs on cast pointer values, e.g. '(u32) result->ef_DAT_file | (u32) result->effDataTable_name', which is a common codegen-sensitive tactic.
Used early returns and variable scoping changes in efAsync_8006737C to improve matching while preserving behavior.
Some functions were deliberately reverted or left NonMatching when build or match quality was insufficient, based on commit summaries for lbrefract.c, ty/toy.c, and ftKirby.

review_feedback.feedback: PR was closed because the batch was too large; author planned smaller focused PRs of 5-7 functions each.
lb review feedback requested use of M2C_FIELD and bool return type.
vi1202.c had review feedback addressed, but the excerpt does not include the exact reviewer comments.
No inline review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided context.

follow_up_queries: Find smaller follow-up PRs from itsgrimetime after PR #2041 that resubmitted these functions in focused batches.
Search for PRs mentioning Camera_8002F9E4 or Camera_8002FC7C to see which camera matches were eventually merged.
Search for M2C_FIELD and bool return type review feedback in lb-related PRs derived from this batch.
Search for vi1202.c follow-up PRs addressing review feedback and matching un_80321AF4 or related un_80321xxx functions.
Audit whether the orig/GALE01/sys symlink appeared only in this closed PR or was removed before later submissions.
